evals.prompt: Follow PSR-12 coding standards in PHP
evals.expected_output: 4-space indentation; opening braces on same line for functions/methods; one class per file; use statements at top after namespace; run PHP CS Fixer with PSR-12 preset to enforce automatically
SRP: one class one responsibility; OCP: extend via interface not modification; DIP: inject dependencies via constructor with interface type-hints; favor composition over inheritance

evals.prompt: Refactor a God class in PHP
evals.expected_output: Identify distinct responsibilities; extract each into its own focused class; use interfaces for dependencies; inject via constructor; test each extracted class independently; keep under ~200 lines

assertions.value: extract each into its own focused class

evals.prompt: Use guard clauses instead of nested if/else in PHP
evals.expected_output: Return early for invalid/exceptional conditions: if (!$user) return null; if (!$this->isValid($data)) throw new InvalidArgumentException(); reduces nesting depth; no else after return

evals.prompt: Extract repetitive logic into PHP traits
evals.expected_output: Define trait HasTimestamps with shared methods; use HasTimestamps; in classes that need it; keep traits focused on one behavior; prefer composition (traits/interfaces) over deep inheritance chains

evals.prompt: Naming conventions for PHP methods and constants
evals.expected_output: Classes: PascalCase (UserService); methods/variables: camelCase (getUserById); class constants: SNAKE_CASE (MAX_RETRIES); file names match class name; namespaces mirror directory structure

should_not_trigger: SOLID principles in a Java Spring application
Clean code in Go with gofmt
